title: Remove Nth node from the back of the LL

content.problemStatement: Given a linked list and an integer N, the task is to delete the Nth node from the end of the linked list and print the updated linked list.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a linked list and an integer N. The linked list will be represented as an array of integers, and N will be a single integer.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the updated linked list after deleting the Nth node from the end.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Two pass approach, first to count the number of nodes and then to delete the Nth node from the end.

code.Python3:
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data1, next1=None):
        self.data = data1
        self.next = next1

class Solution:
    # Function to print the linked list
    def printLL(self, head):
        while head:
            print(head.data, end=" ")
            head = head.next

    # Function to delete the Nth node from the end
    def deleteNthNodeFromEnd(self, head, N):
        if head is None: return None

        cnt = 0
        temp = head

        # Count the number of nodes in the linked list
        while temp:
            cnt += 1
            temp = temp.next

        # If N equals the total number of nodes, delete the head
        if cnt == N:
            return head.next

        # Calculate the position from start to delete
        res = cnt - N
        temp = head

        # Traverse to the node just before the one to delete
        while temp:
            res -= 1
            if res == 0: break
            temp = temp.next

        # Delete the target node
        temp.next = temp.next.next

        return head

if __name__ == "__main__":
    head = Node(1)
    head.next = Node(2)
    head.next.next = Node(3)
    head.next.next.next = Node(4)
    head.next.next.next.next = Node(5)

    N = 3
    sol = Solution()
    head = sol.deleteNthNodeFromEnd(head, N)
    sol.printLL(head)
